title: Sophia Outbound and Service Inbound Plan
summary: This document outlines two action plans: 'Sophia Outbound Follow-Up' for recovering unengaged sales leads and 'Sophia Inbound Service Scheduler' for automating service call handling and appointment booking. It details data integration, operational workflows, product definitions, pricing, and marketing strategies for both AI-driven solutions.
text:
-----------------------------------------
🚀 ACTION PLAN #1 — SOPHIA OUTBOUND FOLLOW-UP (Sales)
“Recovering Unengaged Leads for Dealers”

PHASE 1 — DATA COLLECTION & INTEGRATION
Step 1 — Define “Unengaged Lead” Criteria
Sophia should ONLY call when:
No dealer outbound activity within 72 hours

No customer reply

Status NOT “working,” “appointment,” “sold,” “bad,” or “do-not-contact”

Customer is not suppressed (bad number, opt-out, etc.)

Outcome: A universal safety rule for all rooftops.

Step 2 — Build Tiered Data Ingestion Pipeline
Tier 1 — CRM API (Strategic Path)
Pull activity history, last contact dates, statuses, and opt-outs.
Tier 2 — Automated Daily CRM Export (Fastest Practical Path)
Dealer schedules an auto-export report (CSV/XML) containing:
Lead ID

Last activity timestamp

Customer reply timestamp

Status

Contact info
 We ingest it daily.

Tier 3 — Strolid Engagement Portal (Fallback)
Dealer marks leads as engaged/not engaged manually.
Outcome: A reliable method for filtering leads before Sophia calls.

Step 3 — Confirm Suppression Logic in Strolid
Opt-outs

Bad numbers

Bought elsewhere

Not in market

Outcome: Fully compliant outbound calling.

PHASE 2 — SOPHIA OPERATIONAL WORKFLOW
Step 4 — Establish Standard Cadence
Use the same cadence that works in zBDC:
Day 3 → First Sophia call

Day 4

Day 5

Day 7

Day 10

Day 15
 All captured in vCon → stored in Strolid.


Step 5 — Set Up Callback Routing
Two choices:
Dealer handles call backs

Strolid handles call backs (premium SKU)

Outcome: Dealer-specific routing improves adoption.

Step 6 — Set Reporting Expectations
Provide dealers with:
Calls placed

Voicemails

Live answers

Appointments set

Suppression reasons

Opt-outs

Recovered opportunities

Outcome: Clear value demonstration.

PHASE 3 — PRODUCT DEFINITION + PRICING
Product 1 — Lead Resurrection (One-Time Blast)
Purpose: Show quick wins from old leads.
 Pricing:
AI outbound calls: $0.40/call

Human callbacks: $2.00/call

Appointments set: $15 each (optional)


Product 2 — Always-On Recovery (Recurring Follow-Up)
Your long-term revenue product.
Option A — Fixed Monthly Subscription
$1,500/mo (up to 1,000 leads)

$2,000/mo (1,000–3,000 leads)

Enterprise pricing above 3,000

Option B — Usage-Based
$0.35/call

$2.00 human handoff

$10–$20 per appointment

Option C — Hybrid (Most Likely Winner)
$999 base

$0.30/call

Human backstop included or $1.50/call


PHASE 4 — MARKETING & GTM STRATEGY
Step 7 — Core Messaging Pillars
“We only call leads your team never reached.”

“Sophia recovers deals hiding in your CRM.”

“Customers prefer humans 50% of the time — we built that in.”

“Sophia turns old leads into new appointments.”


Step 8 — Launch Offers
Free Lead Audit

Lead Resurrection Campaign

Always-On Recovery Subscription


Step 9 — GTM Materials Needed
Dealer one-pager

Pricing sheet

Website landing page

PowerPoint slides

“How Sophia Works With Any CRM” diagram

Case study using your 8,400 → 890 → 115 appointment data


-----------------------------------------
🚀 ACTION PLAN #2 — SOPHIA INBOUND SERVICE SCHEDULER (Service)
“AI Answers Your Service Calls and Books Appointments”

PHASE 1 — DATA COLLECTION & INTEGRATION
Step 1 — Identify Service Scheduling Data Needed
Sophia must have:
Access to service scheduler integrations (Vida provides this)

Dealer hours, menus, RO types

Shop capacity rules (if provided)

Contact history (optional but preferred)

Outcome: Sophia can book accurate appointments.

Step 2 — Confirm Vida Integration Workflows
Vida provides:
DMS/CRM connections

Scheduling integrations

Existing OEM-compliant workflows

Strolid adds:
Human escape hatch

vCon conversation intelligence

Reporting

Outcome: Clear division of responsibilities.

PHASE 2 — OPERATIONAL WORKFLOW
Step 3 — Define Call Flow
Inbound call →
 → AI answers →
 → Identifies customer →
 → Books appointment / reschedules / answers questions →
 → Escalates to human when needed →
 → Stores vCon →
 → Pushes appointment into scheduler

Step 4 — Set the Human Escape Hatch Rules
Escalate when:
Customer asks for a person

Customer is confused

Customer requests something AI cannot answer

Complex service questions arise

Outcome: Differentiates Strolid from every other AI service scheduler.

Step 5 — Define Reporting
Calls answered

Booked appointments

Transfers to humans

Customer sentiment

Drop/hang-up reduction


PHASE 3 — PRODUCT + PRICING
Product 1 — AI-Only Service Scheduler
$1,000-$2000/month BASED ON VALUME

Unlimited calls

Unlimited appointments

vCons included

Best for: small stores or low call volume.

Product 2 — AI + Human Escape Hatch
$2,000/month

Unlimited AI

Human escalation included

Best for: medium/large service departments.


PHASE 4 — MARKETING & GTM STRATEGY
Step 6 — Messaging Pillars
“50% of service customers prefer a human — we built that in.”

“Never miss another service call.”

“AI answers immediately. Humans step in when needed.”

“Book more appointments without adding advisors.”


Step 7 — Key Launch Offers
Free analysis of missed service calls

30-day pilot (optional)

Bundle discount with outbound follow-up


Step 8 — Marketing Assets Needed
Dealer-facing one-pager

Pricing sheet

GTM brochure for Vida partnership

Case study showing call-volume reduction + appointments added

Demo video
